How to Force Android Phones onto LTE‑Only or 5G‑Only Networks – UBOS Guide


You can force your Android phone onto LTE‑only or 5G‑only mode by adjusting hidden network settings, using ADB commands, or applying a simple tutorial that modifies the carrier configuration.

How to Force Your Android Phone onto LTE/5G – A Complete Android Police‑Inspired Tutorial


Why Force LTE/5G?

Android enthusiasts often discover that their devices automatically switch between 4G, LTE, and 5G based on signal strength, battery optimization, or carrier policies. While this dynamic behavior is convenient for most users, power users and developers sometimes need a stable, high‑speed connection for tasks like streaming, gaming, or testing network‑dependent apps. The original Android Police tutorial sparked a wave of interest, and we’ve expanded on it with additional context, safety tips, and ways UBOS can streamline the process.

Step‑by‑Step Summary of the Android Network Tutorial

The core of the tutorial revolves around three main actions: enabling developer options, using ADB (Android Debug Bridge) to modify the preferred network mode, and optionally editing the carrier configuration file. Below is a MECE‑structured breakdown.

Enable Developer Options & USB Debugging

  • Open Settings → About phone and tap Build number seven times.
  • Return to System → Developer options and toggle USB debugging on.

Install & Set Up ADB on Your PC

  1. Download the Android platform‑tools package.
  2. Extract the folder and add it to your system PATH.
  3. Connect your phone via USB and verify the connection with adb devices.

Query Current Network Mode

adb shell settings get global preferred_network_mode

The output is a numeric code (e.g., 0 = GSM only, 1 = WCDMA only, 2 = LTE only, 3 = LTE/5G combined).

Force LTE‑Only Mode

adb shell settings put global preferred_network_mode 2

Replace 2 with 3 to force a combined LTE/5G mode, or 4 for 5G‑only on supported devices.

(Optional) Edit Carrier Configuration

Some carriers lock the network mode. Advanced users can pull the carrier_config.xml file, edit the carrier_allowed_network_types entry, and push it back. This step requires root access and is not recommended for casual users.

Verify the Change

adb shell dumpsys telephony.registry | grep mNetworkType

The output should reflect the forced mode (e.g., LTE or NR (5G)).

Once completed, your device will stay on the selected network type until you revert the setting or restart the phone.

Benefits & Risks of Forcing LTE/5G

Benefits

  • Consistent high‑speed data: Ideal for video calls, cloud gaming, and large file transfers.
  • Reduced latency: 5G‑only mode can cut round‑trip times dramatically.
  • Better battery predictability: Some users report steadier battery drain when the radio isn’t constantly switching.
  • Testing environment: Developers can reliably reproduce network conditions for app debugging.

Risks

  • Coverage gaps: If LTE or 5G isn’t available, you may lose connectivity entirely.
  • Potential carrier violations: Some carriers consider forced modes a breach of service terms.
  • Battery impact: In weak signal areas, staying on a high‑power radio can drain the battery faster.
  • System instability: Incorrect carrier config edits can cause crashes or reboot loops.

How UBOS Can Simplify Network Configuration & Automation

While the manual ADB method works, UBOS offers a suite of tools that automate and secure the process, especially for enterprises and power users who manage fleets of devices.

Workflow Automation Studio

Use the Workflow automation studio to create a one‑click script that runs the ADB commands across multiple devices. This eliminates repetitive typing and reduces human error.

UBOS Platform Overview

The UBOS platform overview includes device management modules that can push network settings remotely, perfect for IT admins overseeing corporate Android fleets.

AI Marketing Agents for Device Promotion

If you’re a mobile carrier or OEM, the AI marketing agents can generate personalized campaigns highlighting the benefits of LTE‑only or 5G‑only modes, driving user adoption.

Web App Editor on UBOS

Build a custom web interface for your users with the Web app editor on UBOS. Provide a friendly UI that triggers the network‑forcing script with a single button.

Solutions for Startups & SMBs

Startups can leverage UBOS for startups to prototype network‑control features quickly, while SMBs benefit from UBOS solutions for SMBs that include device compliance monitoring.

Enterprise AI Platform

Large organizations looking for a unified approach should explore the Enterprise AI platform by UBOS, which integrates with existing MDM solutions and adds AI‑driven analytics on network performance.

Templates for Quick Start

Jump‑start your project with ready‑made templates such as the UBOS templates for quick start. For network‑related use cases, the AI SEO Analyzer template can be repurposed to monitor signal strength trends across regions.

Additional AI‑Powered Tools

Explore complementary AI services in the UBOS marketplace that enhance your workflow:

  • AI Article Copywriter – generate documentation for your network scripts.
  • AI YouTube Comment Analysis tool – gauge community feedback on LTE/5G performance.
  • GPT‑Powered Telegram Bot – receive real‑time alerts when devices drop out of LTE/5G.

Pricing, Support, and Community Resources

UBOS offers transparent pricing plans that scale from individual developers to enterprise deployments. All plans include access to the UBOS partner program, which provides dedicated technical support, co‑marketing opportunities, and early access to new AI modules.

Conclusion: Take Control of Your Android Network Today

For tech‑savvy smartphone users and Android enthusiasts, forcing LTE‑only or 5G‑only mode can unlock a smoother, faster, and more predictable mobile experience. By following the concise steps above—or by leveraging UBOS’s automation and AI tools—you can safely apply these changes across single devices or entire fleets.

Ready to automate your network configuration? Visit the UBOS homepage and explore how our platform can accelerate your workflow.
